# TOKIO-REPLACE Dependency-Aware Roadmap Baseline

**Bead**: `asupersync-2oh2u.1.3.3` ([T1.3.c])
**Generated by**: SapphireHill (claude-code / opus-4.6)
**Date**: 2026-03-03
**Dependencies**: T1.3.b (wave classification), T1.3.a (scoring rubric),
T1.1.c (risk register), T1.1.a (inventory)
**Purpose**: Canonical dependency-aware roadmap for the TOKIO-REPLACE program.
Serves as the primary planning input for `bv` triage and per-agent bead
selection. All tracks sequence from this document.

---

## 1. Program Structure

### 1.1 Track Overview

| Track | Bead Prefix | Domain | Gap Coverage | Entry Task |
|-------|-------------|--------|-------------|------------|
| T1 | 2oh2u.1.* | Baseline & DoD | — | **CLOSED** (see `docs/tokio_functional_parity_contract.md` section "Domain Definition-of-Done Synthesis (T1.2 Parent)") |
| T2 | 2oh2u.2.* | Async I/O & tokio-util | G11 | T2.1 |
| T3 | 2oh2u.3.* | Filesystem / Process / Signal | G8, G12, G13 | T3.1 |
| T4 | 2oh2u.4.* | QUIC / HTTP/3 | G1, G2 | T4.1 |
| T5 | 2oh2u.5.* | Web / Middleware / gRPC | G7 | T5.1 |
| T6 | 2oh2u.6.* | Database / Messaging | G5, G6, G9, G10 | T6.1 |
| T7 | 2oh2u.7.* | Tokio Interop | G3, G4 | T7.1 |
| T8 | 2oh2u.10.* | Determinism / Conformance / CI | — | T8.1 |
| T9 | 2oh2u.11.* | Migration / Release / GA | — | T9.1 |

### 1.2 Track Priority Mapping

| Priority | Tracks | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| **Critical** | T7 (Interop), T4 (QUIC/H3) | R4 risk, replacement-claim blockers |
| **High** | T5 (Web/gRPC), T6 (DB/Messaging), T3 (FS/Process) | P1 gaps, high user impact |
| **Medium** | T2 (I/O), T8 (Conformance) | P2 gaps, quality hardening |
| **Standard** | T9 (Migration/Release) | Sequential, follows implementation |

---

## 2. Cross-Track Dependency Graph

```
T1 (Baseline) ─── CLOSED ──→ T2.1, T3.1, T4.1, T5.1, T6.1, T7.1, T8.1, T9.1
                               │      │      │      │      │      │      │     │
                               ▼      ▼      ▼      ▼      ▼      ▼      ▼     ▼
                             T2.2   T3.2   T4.2   T5.2   T6.2   T7.2   T8.2  T9.2
                               │      │      │      │      │      │      │     │
                              ...    ...     │     ...    ...     │     ...   ...
                                             │                    │
                                             ▼                    ▼
T4 (QUIC) ──── T4.2 ───────→ T4.5 (H3) ──→ T4.6 (interop matrix)
                                             │
T7 (Interop) ─ T7.2 → T7.4 ──────────────→ T7.7 (conformance suites)
                                             │
                                    ┌────────┘
                                    ▼
T8 (Conformance) ─ T8.2 → T8.4 → T8.5 → T8.8 → T8.9 (readiness gate)
                                                    │
                                                    ▼
T9 (Migration) ── T9.2 → T9.5 → T9.8 (RFC) ────→ T9.9 (GA review)
```

### 2.1 Critical Path to Replacement Claim

The minimum path to the final replacement claim RFC (T9.8):

```
T1.3.c (this doc) → T7.1 → T7.2 → T7.4 → T7.7 ──┐
                   → T4.1 → T4.2 → T4.5 → T4.6 ──┤
                                                    ▼
                                              T8.9 (readiness gate)
                                                    │
                                                    ▼
                                              T9.8 (replacement RFC)
                                                    │
                                                    ▼
                                              T9.9 (GA review)
```

**Critical path length**: ~12-16 tasks, estimated 10-14 weeks with parallelism.

### 2.2 Cross-Track Integration Points

| Integration Point | Producing Track | Consuming Tracks | Milestone |
|-------------------|-----------------|------------------|-----------|
| Interop MVP | T7 (T7.4) | T5, T6 (conditional gap elimination) | M2 |
| QUIC transport MVP | T4 (T4.2) | T4.5 (H3), T5 (gRPC-over-QUIC) | M3 |
| Conformance contracts | T8 (T8.2) | All implementation tracks | M1 |
| Readiness gate | T8 (T8.9) | T9 (migration/release) | M5 |
| Migration cookbooks | T9 (T9.2) | External (users) | M6 |

---

## 3. Execution Phases

### Phase A — Foundation (Weeks 1-2)

**Objective**: Unblock all tracks by completing entry-point audits.

| Task | Track | Gap | Assignable | Est. |
|------|-------|-----|-----------|------|
| T2.1 Complete I/O parity audit | T2 | G11 | Yes | 2-3 days |
| T3.1 Build fs/process/signal matrix | T3 | G8, G12, G13 | Yes | 2-3 days |
| T4.1 Unpark QUIC/H3 features | T4 | G1, G2 | Yes | 2-3 days |
| T5.1 Build web/gRPC parity map | T5 | G7 | Yes | 2-3 days |
| T6.1 Build DB/messaging gap baseline | T6 | G5, G6, G9, G10 | Yes | 2-3 days |
| T7.1 Rank interop targets | T7 | G3 | Yes | 2-3 days |
| T8.1 Expand lab models | T8 | — | Yes | 3-5 days |
| T9.1 Define migration strategy | T9 | — | Yes | 2-3 days |

**Parallelism**: All 8 tasks are independent. With 4-5 agents, Phase A
completes in ~3-4 days.

**Exit criteria**: All T*.1 tasks closed with published audit/matrix documents.

---

### Phase B — Architecture & Core Implementation (Weeks 2-6)

**Objective**: Resolve R4 critical gaps and build core infrastructure.

#### B.1 — Interop Track (Critical Path)

| Order | Task | Bead | Dependencies | Est. |
|-------|------|------|-------------|------|
| 1 | Define adapter boundary architecture | T7.2 | T7.1 | 3-5 days |
| 2 | Implement compat crate scaffolding | T7.3 | T7.2 | 3-5 days |
| 3 | Implement runtime/task/time/io adapters | T7.4 | T7.2 | 1-2 weeks |
| 4 | Add tower/hyper/tonic boundary adapters | T7.5 | T7.4 | 1 week |
| 5 | Implement blocking bridge & context propagation | T7.6 | T7.4 | 1 week |

**Decision point at T7.4 completion**: Evaluate which downstream gaps (G4, G5,
G9, G10) are eliminated by the interop layer. Re-score affected gaps.

#### B.2 — QUIC Track (Long Lead Time)

| Order | Task | Bead | Dependencies | Est. |
|-------|------|------|-------------|------|
| 1 | Complete QUIC transport core | T4.2 | T4.1 | 3-5 weeks |
| 2 | Finalize flow/congestion control | T4.3 | T4.2 | 1-2 weeks |
| 3 | Implement 0-RTT, resumption, migration | T4.4 | T4.2 | 1-2 weeks |

**Note**: T4.2-T4.4 can partially overlap. T4.5 (HTTP/3) cannot start until
T4.2 reaches a transport MVP.

#### B.3 — Parallel Implementation Tracks

These run concurrently with B.1 and B.2:

| Task | Track | Gap | Dependencies | Est. |
|------|-------|-----|-------------|------|
| T2.2 Close AsyncRead/AsyncWrite gaps | T2 | G11 | T2.1 | 3-5 days |
| T3.2 Close core filesystem API gaps | T3 | G8 | T3.1 | 3-5 days |
| T5.2 Close router/route-matching gaps | T5 | G7 | T5.1 | 3-5 days |
| T6.2 Harden PostgreSQL client | T6 | G9 | T6.1 | 1 week |
| T8.2 Define conformance contracts | T8 | — | T8.1 | 1 week |

**Phase B exit criteria**:
- T7.4 (interop adapters) complete and tested
- T4.2 (QUIC transport) reaches MVP
- All T*.2 tasks closed

---

### Phase C — Hardening & Integration (Weeks 5-9)

**Objective**: Close P1 gaps, prove cancellation correctness, and harden all
implementation tracks.

| Task | Track | Focus | Dependencies |
|------|-------|-------|-------------|
| T2.3-T2.5 | T2 | I/O utilities, codecs, cancel proofs | T2.2 |
| T3.3-T3.6 | T3 | Advanced FS, process lifecycle, cancel proofs | T3.2 |
| T4.5 | T4 | HTTP/3 protocol layer | T4.2 MVP |
| T5.3-T5.6 | T5 | Extractors, middleware, gRPC, lifecycle | T5.2 |
| T6.3-T6.5 | T6 | MySQL, SQLite, pool contracts | T6.2 |
| T7.7 | T7 | Interop conformance suites | T7.4 |
| T8.4-T8.5 | T8 | Fuzz campaigns, CI gates | T8.2 |

**Phase C decision point**: At ~50% completion, evaluate:
1. Which gaps have been closed by G3 interop layer?
2. Is QUIC transport on track for HTTP/3 unblock?
3. Are any tracks stalled that need resource reallocation?

**Phase C exit criteria**:
- All P1 gaps (G3, G4, G7, G9) closed or eliminated
- Cancellation correctness proofs for all async domains
- CI quality gates operational

---

### Phase D — Messaging, Polish & Evidence (Weeks 8-12)

**Objective**: Close remaining P2/P3 gaps, complete messaging, and produce
evidence artifacts.

| Task | Track | Focus | Dependencies |
|------|-------|-------|-------------|
| T6.6-T6.8 | T6 | Redis, NATS, Kafka clients | T6.5 |
| T4.6-T4.8 | T4 | QUIC/H3 interop, soak tests, observability | T4.5 |
| T5.7-T5.8 | T5 | gRPC production features, interop suites | T5.6 |
| T3.7 | T3 | Deterministic conformance suites | T3.6 |
| T2.6-T2.8 | T2 | Reactor hardening, conformance gates | T2.5 |
| T8.6-T8.8 | T8 | Replay schema, regression budgets, security audits | T8.5 |

**Opportunistic work** (do if capacity allows):
- G5 (AMQP) — only if not eliminated by G3 interop
- G6 (MQTT) — only if IoT vertical is prioritized
- G12 (Windows signals) — batch with T3 platform work
- G13 (Process PTY) — batch with T3 process work

**Phase D exit criteria**:
- All P2 gaps closed or documented with waivers
- All evidence categories (E1-E8) populated for completed domains
- Performance benchmarks baseline established

---

### Phase E — Release Preparation & GA (Weeks 11-14)

**Objective**: Produce migration materials, execute readiness gate, and author
replacement claim.

| Order | Task | Track | Dependencies |
|-------|------|-------|-------------|
| 1 | T8.9 Build readiness gate aggregator | T8 | T8.8, all track closures |
| 2 | T9.2 Publish migration cookbooks | T9 | T8.9 |
| 3 | T9.3 Publish compatibility matrix | T9 | T9.2 |
| 4 | T9.4 Deliver reference applications | T9 | T9.3 |
| 5 | T9.5 Define release channels | T9 | T9.3 |
| 6 | T9.7 External validation | T9 | T9.5 |
| 7 | T9.8 Author replacement claim RFC | T9 | T8.9, T9.5 |
| 8 | T9.9 Execute GA review | T9 | T9.8 |

**Phase E is sequential** — each step depends on the previous. Limited
parallelism within this phase.

**Phase E exit criteria**:
- T9.9 closed: GA readiness checklist passes
- Final replacement claim RFC signed

---

## 4. Milestone Definitions

| ID | Milestone | Target | Gate Criteria |
|----|-----------|--------|---------------|
| M0 | Baseline complete | Week 0 | T1 fully closed (DONE) |
| M1 | Audits complete | Week 2 | All T*.1 entry tasks closed |
| M2 | Interop MVP | Week 5 | T7.4 closed, gap re-scoring complete |
| M3 | QUIC transport MVP | Week 6 | T4.2 closed, H3 unblocked |
| M4 | P1 gaps closed | Week 9 | G3, G7, G9 at R1; G4 eliminated or R1 |
| M5 | Readiness gate | Week 12 | T8.9 passes, all R4→R1 transitions confirmed |
| M6 | Replacement claim | Week 14 | T9.8 signed, T9.9 reviewed |

### Milestone Decision Points

| Milestone | Decision | If Yes | If No |
|-----------|----------|--------|-------|
| M2 | Does interop eliminate G4, G5, G9, G10? | Drop those gaps, reallocate agents | Build native implementations |
| M3 | Is QUIC transport production-ready? | Start H3 immediately | Extend T4 by 2 weeks |
| M4 | Are all P1 gaps closed? | Proceed to Phase D | Extend Phase C, add agents |
| M5 | Does readiness gate pass? | Start Phase E | Fix blockers, re-run gate |

---

## 5. Agent Allocation Strategy

### Recommended Agent-to-Track Mapping

| Agent Count | Track Assignment |
|-------------|-----------------|
| 1-2 agents | T7 (interop — critical path), T4 (QUIC — long lead) |
| 3-4 agents | Add T5 (web/gRPC), T6 (DB/messaging) |
| 5-6 agents | Add T2 (I/O), T3 (filesystem) |
| 7+ agents | Add T8 (conformance), T9 (migration) |

### Quick Wins for Idle Agents

These tasks can be completed in <1 day during wait periods:

| Task | Gap | Effort | Impact |
|------|-----|--------|--------|
| G11 I/O adapters (ReaderStream, StreamReader) | G11 | <4 hours | Closes P2 gap |
| G8 convenience ops (symlink, copy, canonicalize) | G8 partial | <1 day | Reduces P2 gap |
| G12 Windows signal handler | G12 | 1-2 days | Closes P3 gap |

---

## 6. Risk Mitigations

| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-------------|--------|------------|
| G3 interop infeasible (Tokio Context thread-local) | Medium | Critical | Early spike (T7.2), fallback to per-crate shims |
| QUIC implementation exceeds 8 weeks | Medium | High | Scope to connection+stream MVP, defer 0-RTT |
| H2 compile errors block CI | High | Medium | Already tracked; agent in-flight fix |
| Beads DB contention during multi-agent work | High | Low | Use `--no-db`, coordinate via agent mail |
| Evidence collection bottleneck at Phase E | Medium | Medium | Start E6/E7 docs incrementally in each phase |

---

## 7. Progress Tracking

### Per-Track Status Template

Agents should update track status using this format in agent mail:

```
Track: T<N>
Phase: A/B/C/D/E
Tasks closed: X / Y
Current task: T<N>.<M>
Blockers: [list or "none"]
Next milestone: M<K>
```

### bv Triage Integration

This roadmap is the canonical input for `bv --robot-triage`. Track priorities:
1. Any unblocked task in T7 (interop) — highest priority
2. Any unblocked task in T4 (QUIC) — second priority
3. Any unblocked task in T5/T6 — third priority
4. Quick wins (G11, G8 partial, G12) — opportunistic
5. All other tracks — standard priority

### Re-Roadmap Triggers

This roadmap should be re-baselined when:
- M2 (Interop MVP) decision point reached
- Any R4 risk remains unresolved past Week 8
- Agent count changes by ≥2 (rebalance allocation)
- A new critical gap is discovered
